A.text: Two pentagons share a common edge.

B.text: No two hexagons share a common edge.

C.text: Each pentagon is adjacent only to hexagons.

D.text: Each hexagon is adjacent only to pentagons.

parsed.answer_value: Each pentagon is adjacent only to hexagons.
parsed.rationale: The image shows a truncated icosahedron, which is the structure of a buckyball (C60). In this structure, every pentagonal face is surrounded by hexagonal faces. This means each pentagon shares its edges exclusively with hexagons. Conversely, each hexagon shares edges with both pentagons and other hexagons. Therefore, option C accurately describes the adjacency pattern in this molecule.
